rationale: The signal set is heavily dominated by Google autocomplete and YouTube, with strong volume around three specific morphs: cappuccino (8 autocomplete variants, 13 videos), lilly white (9 autocomplete variants, 3 recent videos), and axanthic (8 autocomplete variants). No forum heat, Bluesky activity, or Google Trends data emerged. The autocomplete and YouTube signals cluster around identification, breeding logistics, and genetics—not general husbandry. However, three critical facts constrain my recommendations: (1) cappuccino-related topics are already heavily published (cappuccino-crested-gecko-genetics, super-cappuccino-viability-debate, super-cappuccino-breeding-risks, cappuccino-pairing-risks-runts); (2) lilly-white-identification-breeding is already queued; (3) axanthic-breeding-strategy is already queued. YouTube videos like "Don't buy super cappuccino crested gecko it's a scam and waste of money" and "Why So Much Hate for Cappuccino Geckos?" hint at real breeder sentiment, but we lack forum threads or primary sources to ground a fresh angle. The only unblocked signal is soft-scale (1 autocomplete mention, 1 YouTube sidebar), but it lacks sufficient demand. With no unique demand signal, no forum disagreement, and three already-published/queued morphs dominating the feed, I am not recommending any topics this week.
